expect: Identify >=3 falsified frontiers lacking lesson follow-up; lesson conversion rate <60%
actual: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. 22 frontiers explicitly falsified across repo. 18/22 (81.8%) have lesson follow-up — BETTER than predicted <60%. 3 specific gaps found, not >=3 WITHOUT follow-up but 3 with degraded follow-up: (1) F-FLT2 FALSIFIED S433 with zero lesson, (2) L-1424 phantom reference in F-FLT7, (3) L-1194 archived but still cited as if active in F-RAND1.
diff: Expected >=3 missing and <60% rate. Got 3 gaps but 81.8% rate (prediction direction WRONG). The falsification-to-lesson pipeline works well — the gap is narrow and specific, not systemic. F-EVAL2's real problem is external grounding (0% strict), not internal lesson coverage.

gaps.issue: FALSIFIED S433, no lesson documenting result
gaps.finding: All 3 proxy metrics improved at scale; BLIND-SPOT accessibility is true concern

gaps.issue: References L-1424 which does not exist (phantom lesson ID)
gaps.finding: L-1422 covers related work but L-1424 was never written

gaps.issue: L-1194 archived but still cited as active in frontier notes
gaps.finding: L-1194 in archive, L-1177 is the active replacement

results.structural_finding: F-EVAL2 real gap is external grounding (0% strict, ~5% generous) not internal lesson coverage. 529 sessions, 0 resolved external validations. The swarm documents its falsifications well but never tests against outside reality.

surprise: HIGH — internal pipeline much better than expected; external gap much worse
successor: Fix F-FLT2 lesson gap. Clean phantom L-1424 reference. Track external grounding ratio trend.
